repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ot_dte_marginal/v1 pursued an unproved marginal-envelope pasting frontier, not this response-type map.

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot — Proposition 3.1 gives a sharp generalized-Roy latent-law characterization, but not this finite joint-potential-outcome response-type table.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_poc_tau_band_concordance_sharp_dr is a conditional-copula population target; pid_ot_dte_marginal/v1 is a marginal-envelope DTE path problem.

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot treats generalized-Roy CCOT with product costs; ober_reynolds_2023_joint_po_ot treats joint-potential-outcome OT under the LATE/complier setup, not this Roy response-type coupling.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives dual-score inference for a finite generalized-Roy joint-coupling LP.

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot and ober_reynolds_2023_joint_po_ot provide the closest DML/dual-OT inference templates; fang_santos_2019_directional_inference supplies the nonsmooth resampling framework.
tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot — its central reduction is exactly the product/separable-cost regime.

novelty_flags.label: firpo_ridder_2019_ted_functionals
novelty_flags.one_line: The bibliography calls the paper 'The Average and Quantile Effects of a Binary Treatment on a Distribution,' but the cited DOI is Firpo--Ridder (2019), 'Partial identification of the treatment effect distribution and its functionals'; correct the title before relying on its pointwise-versus-uniform-sharpness result.

soundness_flags.label: ass:cutpoint-saturated-design / thm:factual-map-pasting
soundness_flags.one_line: The inclusion {q_0,...,q_K}⊆{p_mℓ} permits additional p_mℓ strictly inside an I_k, where A_m omits the partially treated cell although P(D=1,Y=y_h|Z=z_ℓ,X=x_m) includes it; require every p_mℓ to be a partition endpoint (or refine the map).

soundness_flags.label: ass:roy-rank / oeq:sharp-common-coupling
soundness_flags.one_line: G_m and g_m are never specified as concrete generalized-Roy restrictions, yet the claimed binary interval ignores their possible exclusion of feasible couplings, including t=1.

soundness_flags.label: thm:dual-inference
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated estimator and score do not specify an asymptotic-linear nuisance estimator or a correcting moment; adding the endpoint LP derivative score to a sample LP value can double-count its first-order perturbation, so orthogonality and the CLT do not follow from the listed assumptions.

soundness_flags.label: prop:separable-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Removing cross-(i,j) restrictions only from H_ε does not imply separability while G_m r_m≤g_m may still constrain the joint (i,j) table; the proposition needs the analogous condition on every non-factual constraint.

soundness_flags.label: ass:roy-rank
soundness_flags.one_line: The cited Tan--Blanchet--Syrgkanis generalized-Roy paper formulates CCOT from threshold-selection and observed-law constraints, not an unspecified 'finite-grid generalized-Roy rank restriction' G_mr_m≤g_m; define and source the restriction or retag it novel.

soundness_flags.label: ass:nuisance-product-rate
soundness_flags.one_line: Chernozhukov et al. (2018) supports score-specific remainder/product-rate conditions, not the unrestricted product over an arbitrary Q; with |Q|=1 the displayed condition even demands an o_P(n^-1/2) first-stage rate.

soundness_flags.label: ass:polyhedral-copula
soundness_flags.one_line: An arbitrary analyst-specified H_εr≤h_ε is not yet a credible empirical sensitivity model; the justification's claim that finite odds-ratio restrictions are polyhedral is generally false because odds ratios are nonlinear in r.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:dual-inference
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Inference is conditional on a repaired and affirmatively resolved sharp common-coupling characterization and on the stated regular finite-LP regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Root-n and directional-bootstrap claims apply only conditional on the repaired sharp finite-grid generalized-Roy identified-set result.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Reverse/refine the cutpoint condition so every observed propensity p_mℓ is a partition endpoint, then restate and recheck the factual map for all cells.
Specify G_m,g_m and a concrete copula sensitivity family; add conditions proving that the binary witness rank restrictions leave the full interval [(1+κ)/2,1] feasible.

Replace the current inference statement with an explicit endpoint-value influence expansion and a one-step estimator whose correction cancels, rather than repeats, the LP-value derivative; state score-specific nuisance rates.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version and rebuild the finite design/map and LP regularity core before any Stage 0 derivation; the load-bearing factual-map claim is false under the stated saturation assumption.
